body.title: Postgres or flat-file only — no SQLite, no fallbacks, no dual paths, no mocked DB in proofs
body.statement: Shared state lives in Postgres. Boot-time state that must load before Postgres is up lives in flat files (the skeleton registry pattern). There is no third option: any use of SQLite — import, file creation, in-memory — is unconditionally prohibited (sqlite3, aiosqlite, tinydb, pickleDB, *.db/*.sqlite backends). No 'fallback' file store for state that belongs in Postgres — dual paths split the source of truth. Proof tests exercise the real store, never a mocked DB.
body.why: SQLite under concurrent write load (multiple devices) produces lock contention and silent data loss. A fallback path is a second writer with no owner. A mocked DB in a proof is a test that passes on hollow output — the exact signature proof-on-close exists to catch.
